symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden low back pain and paraspinal spasm after trivial flexion

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute non-specific mechanical low back pain with muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical onset with immediate protective spasm and recumbency-only relief is the hallmark of benign mechanical low back pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: No radiation below the knee, no leg numbness or weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of dermatomal below-knee radiation and neurological deficit makes a compressive radiculopathy unlikely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute non-specific mechanical low back pain with muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain confined to the back without neural signs supports a musculoligamentous, non-specific cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact bladder/bowel control and no saddle anaesthesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continence and normal perineal sensation on history are the key features that make cauda equina syndrome very unlikely; their absence would mandate emergency escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No trauma, fever, night sweats, weight loss or malignancy history

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Trivial flexion mechanism with no trauma and no bony point tenderness makes fracture unlikely.
No constitutional or nocturnal-pain features to suggest infection or metastatic disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Board-like paraspinal rigidity and extreme tenderness on palpation, no midline bony step-off

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute non-specific mechanical low back pain with muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable paraspinal spasm with absent midline bony tenderness — a clinician examination finding, sealed as it cannot be obtained via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal lower-limb neurology: 5/5 power, symmetrical 2+ reflexes, intact light touch, negative bilateral straight leg raise, normal anal tone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal neurological examination definitively excludes an established cauda equina lesion — but this is a clinician exam the AI Doctor cannot perform; it must rely on the history screen (SYM-003) and safety-netting instead.
Negative straight leg raise and intact myotomes/reflexes argue strongly against nerve-root compression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure 140/90, heart rate 96 (mild elevation consistent with acute pain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute non-specific mechanical low back pain with muscle spasm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild elevation attributable to pain and distress; not indicative of shock or a sinister cause. Clinician-measured, sealed — no home device offered.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Benign mechanical spasm with a clean red-flag screen
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute non-specific mechanical low back pain with muscle spasm
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of a mechanical onset with a fully negative cauda equina, radicular and sinister-feature screen is what licenses confident conservative telehealth management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Imaging is NOT indicated for acute non-specific low back pain without red flags. It does not change management, delays recovery through over-medicalisation, and drives incidental-finding cascades. Ordering it here is an error of commission.

Reserved ONLY for the scenario where a cauda equina red flag (RF-001) or a progressive neurological deficit emerges — then it becomes emergency imaging alongside surgical referral. Not applicable while the screen is negative.
